message:
wasm: fuse rms_norm + convert_fp16_to_f32 across the model

Every rms_norm caller in this model is followed immediately by a
convert_fp16_to_f32 of the result; the fp16 normed buffer is never
read otherwise. Adds two SIMD-vectorized exports that collapse the
sequence into one pass:

  rms_norm_fp16_to_f32       — pre-attention norm, final norm
  add_rmsnorm_fp16_to_f32    — post-attention residual+norm; the
                               fp16 sum doubles as residual2

Block now passes the f32-widened hidden directly to attention (which
drops its internal convert + scratch alloc), and the post-attention
add+norm path collapses three kernels + an explicit residual2 memcpy
into a single call per worker in MT mode (and a single call in ST).

Smoke parity vs transformers.js holds (2/2 spans). ST WASM is bench-
neutral — rmsnorm is <1% of forward — but MT mode drops 18 worker
scripts + 1 main-thread memcpy per layer to 6 worker scripts, and
the code is materially simpler (4 fewer scratch buffers per block).
